message:
fix(daemon,guardrails): reduce log noise and improve e-process penalty scaling

Two targeted improvements to production monitoring behavior:

1. Scan channel saturation logging (loop_main.rs):
   - Increase rate-limit from 5min to 1hr — scans routinely take 300-600s
     so "channel saturated" fires nearly every 60s tick during active scanning,
     which is expected behavior not worth logging frequently
   - Track and report suppressed warning count so operators still see volume
   - Add 30-second flush deadline to prevent unbounded post-scan candidate
     processing; remaining candidates are rediscovered on next pass
   - Fix budget_secs calculation: use .min() instead of .max() to cap at 600s
     rather than floor at 600s (Red/Critical) or 300s (Orange)

2. Proportional e-process penalty (guardrails.rs):
   - Scale penalty by how far rate_danger_ratio exceeds the threshold instead
     of applying full penalty for barely-bad observations (ratio=0.31 vs
     threshold=0.30 now gets 30% penalty instead of 100%)
   - Lower upper clamp from 5.0 to 3.5 (exp(5)=148 caused prolonged FAIL
     on Green-pressure machines; exp(3.5)=33 recovers within ~7 good
     observations)
   - Update test expectations to match proportional recovery curve

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
